# tailbus

> Your agents are smart. Now make them a team.

tailbus is an open-source mesh network that lets AI agents discover each other by name and collaborate across machines. One install, no networking code, no infrastructure to manage.

## What it does

- Agents register a handle (e.g. @marketing, @finance, @deploy) and become instantly discoverable
- Any agent can message any other agent by handle — the mesh handles routing, NAT traversal, and encryption
- Works peer-to-peer over gRPC with automatic discovery via a coordination server
- MCP gateway lets Claude, Cursor, and other AI tools call any agent on the mesh as a tool
- Supports the A2A (agent-to-agent) protocol

## Who it's for

- Teams building multiple AI agents that need to collaborate
- Companies running agents across departments (marketing, finance, engineering, etc.)
- Developers who want agent-to-agent communication without building networking infrastructure

## Key links

- Website: https://tailbus.co
- Getting started: https://tailbus.co/getting-started
- GitHub: https://github.com/alexanderfrey/tailbus
- Blog: https://tailbus.co/blog
- Pricing: https://tailbus.co/pricing

## How it works

1. Install tailbus (one command: `curl -sSL https://tailbus.co/install | sh`)
2. Start the daemon (`tailbusd`) and authenticate with Google OAuth
3. Register agents with handles using the Python SDK (`pip install tailbus`)
4. Agents discover and message each other by handle — no endpoints, no auth tokens, no routing config

## Technical details

- Two binaries: `tailbusd` (daemon) and `tailbus` (CLI)
- Python SDK: `tailbus` on PyPI
- Encrypted gRPC for agent-to-agent communication
- NAT traversal built in (works behind firewalls, on laptops, across clouds)
- Coordination server for discovery (messages never pass through it)
- Linux and macOS supported
